stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.text:
For AskCallback, I feel like it violates obp the naming, and how it should really be, I think.

I would think it would be like this, 'ask user "name?"' -> this gets funnelled to stream, then if the stream is stateless we sould create the callstack on it and then serialize it, if it is statefull we just serialize it. Lets disucss
stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.anchor: Stage 2a fixes all four for **any** callback kind. `AskCallback` gets a real mid-goal continuation it never had. Stage 2b builds `PermissionAskCallback` on top of this foundation.
stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.author: user

stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.text: I think we can do this, it is only working with stream, not touching on http. plus, isn´t it just serialize just include the callback?
stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.anchor: **Out of scope:** building the HTTP/Web channel. The serializer is generic; whatever channel wires it (CLI today; HTTP when shipped — see `Channel/Message/this.cs:7` "Web extends Message (when shipped)") is a separate branch's concern. Stage 2a makes the bytes correct; the channel can use them whenever it lands.
stage-2a-callback-roundtrip.md.author: user

stage-2a-snapshot-resume.md.text: here we still have AskCallback and ERrorCallback, make sure to fix that in all stage files
stage-2a-snapshot-resume.md.anchor: **Goal:** Unify all suspend/resume flows through `Snapshot`. Today there are two parallel mechanisms — `AskCallback.Run` (variable-bind + re-dispatch) and `ErrorCallback.Run` (App.Restore + dispatch). Both round-trip exists only as scaffolding (zero production callers outside the Wire serializer comment). Replace both with a single rule: when a step returns a Data whose Type "exits the goal", the engine captures a Snapshot, attaches it to the result, and short-circuits the goal. Whoever holds the result (channel, in-process resumer, wire receiver) restores via `App.Restore(snapshot)` and dispatches from the captured Position.
stage-2a-snapshot-resume.md.author: user

stage-2a-snapshot-resume.md.text: AskCore = Ask
stage-2a-snapshot-resume.md.anchor: - **Not a new channel.** Stream channel is the only one whose ask-blocking path is fully wired today (it's the only place that actually exercises the current `AskCore`); that path is what stage 2a exercises end-to-end. The Message/HTTP channel is parked — its `Ask` body lands when HTTP work happens.
stage-2a-snapshot-resume.md.author: user

stage-2a-snapshot-resume.md.text: So we dont need the Exit method, just need to check if Snapshot is null?
stage-2a-snapshot-resume.md.anchor: **Note on `Type.Exit()`:** the predicate (deliverable #1) is still useful — it's the *invariant* the engine and tests assert (Exit-typed result MUST have a Snapshot; non-Exit-typed result MUST NOT). The step loop checks `Snapshot != null` because the action did the actual capture; the predicate gates which actions are allowed to do so.
stage-2a-snapshot-resume.md.author: user

But we cannot do this becuase ask is simple text so for now we need to have BuildRequst and SignAndStore. Lets add to todo to add options to output.ask and this needs to be fixed when it gets added
